attempts.description: Interpreter-owned retry counters keyed by workflow edge.

outputs.description: Legacy mirror of validated structured per-step worker outputs keyed by step id.

^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_-]*$.description: Per-step worker output keyed by workflow step id for downstream input.state projection.

user_prompt.description: Raw run-level startup user prompt captured when the workflow run is initialized. Immutable after start/resume.

user_prompt_injected.description: Interpreter/runner marker set after the selected startup-prompt worker output has been applied. Prevents reinjection after completion/resume or workflow drift while allowing repeated renders of the same uncompleted worker to preserve the prompt.

user_prompt_target.description: Interpreter-owned selected startup worker step id that receives baton.user_prompt before any worker output exists. Stable across resume and workflow order drift.

id.description: Artifact id unique within the producer step.
id.x-use: Emit a stable id unique within the current producer step. Use with the step id for lookup; do not treat it as globally unique by itself.
id.x-read-usage: Use with the projected producer step id to identify this artifact; do not require a separate producer_step_id field.

content_type.description: MIME/content type, for example text/markdown or application/json.
content_type.x-use: Emit the MIME/content type needed to render or parse the file; do not duplicate it with artifact type/kind.
content_type.x-read-usage: Use this to decide how to read or render the artifact file. Do not infer artifact kind from id text.

path.x-use: Emit the run-relative artifact file path under the current step artifacts directory, for example research_draft/artifacts/research-packet.md.
path.x-read-usage: Treat this as the artifact file location relative to the run directory. Do not rewrite it unless producing a new artifact.

summary.description: Optional compact handoff summary for the artifact.
summary.x-use: Use only as compact metadata; the artifact file remains the source of truth for full content.
summary.x-read-usage: Use as orientation before opening the artifact file; do not substitute it for the file content when approval or review needs the full artifact.

ref.description: Optional derived compact locator; normally omitted when id and path are enough.
ref.x-use: Normally omit. If present, keep it a display/lookup convenience derived from step context, id, and path.
ref.x-read-usage: Optional display locator only; prefer path plus projected step id and artifact id when ref is absent.
